diagrams.title: Dynamic Array Memory Model
diagrams.description: Shows how a dynamic array is stored in heap memory with the pointer referencing the first element
elements: Heap memory blocks for array elements [0], [1], [2], [3], [n-1]
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how dynamic arrays are laid out in memory and how pointers reference them

diagrams.title: 2D Dynamic Array: Pointer to Pointers Method
diagrams.description: Illustrates the structure of a 2D array created using int** with separate allocations for row pointers and each row's data
elements: Each row: contiguous block of column elements
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing nested dynamic allocation for 2D arrays and memory management requirements

diagrams.title: Memory Leak vs Normal Deallocation
diagrams.description: Compares a program that properly frees memory versus one with memory leak
elements: Normal: malloc → use → free → memory returned to system
Leak: malloc → use → (no free) → memory remains allocated → heap fills up
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the importance of proper memory deallocation and consequences of forgetting free()
